name: Internal Revenue
description_short: Internal Revenue Employees Federal Credit Union is a member-owned financial institution serving the Greensboro, NC area with comprehensive banking, lending, and investment services.
description_long:
Internal Revenue Employees Federal Credit Union (IREFCU) is a federally chartered credit union serving the Greensboro, North Carolina community and surrounding areas. As a credit union, it operates on a member-owned, not-for-profit basis and is regulated by the National Credit Union Administration (NCUA). The institution focuses on enhancing the financial well-being of its membership through accessible financial products and personalized service.

IREFCU offers a comprehensive suite of financial services including high-yield checking and savings accounts, flexible auto loans (new and used), mortgages, credit cards, and retirement planning services. The credit union provides competitive interest rates, with new auto loans starting at 2.90% and used auto loans also at 2.90%. Members have access to robust online and mobile banking platforms enabling account management, fund transfers, bill pay, and balance inquiries. The institution also offers IRA/KEOGH retirement accounts, brokered certificates of deposit, and dedicated financial planning and wealth management services.

IREFCU distinguishes itself through its credit union structure, which prioritizes member benefits over shareholder profits. The institution provides exclusive member benefits and discounts, personalized service, and competitive rates across its product offerings. With 45 active IRA/KEOGH accounts and 878 savings accounts held by members, the credit union serves a stable membership base. The organization emphasizes accessibility through multiple service channels including in-person branches, online banking, and mobile applications.

As a smaller credit union with limited membership (approximately 100 credit card members, 36+ new auto loan members, 75+ used auto loan members), IREFCU may have restrictions on membership eligibility and could have fewer branch locations than larger financial institutions. The website indicates zero checking accounts held, which appears to be a data anomaly or reporting issue. Prospective members should verify current eligibility requirements and service availability in their area before opening an account. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: New auto loans with rates starting at 2.90%
Used auto loans with rates starting at 2.90%
Online banking with bill pay and fund transfers
Mobile banking application for smartphone access
pros: Competitive auto loan rates starting at 2.90% for both new and used vehicles
Member-owned credit union structure prioritizes member benefits over profits
Comprehensive online and mobile banking with bill pay, transfers, and balance management
IRA/KEOGH retirement accounts with tax-advantaged savings options
Dedicated financial planning and wealth management services available
Brokered certificates of deposit for investment diversification
Credit cards with competitive starting interest rate of 10.90% to build credit
cons: Appears to be a very small credit union with limited membership and potentially restricted eligibility
Limited geographic service area (primarily Greensboro, NC and surrounding areas)
Website shows zero checking accounts, suggesting potential data issues or service limitations
No clear information on membership eligibility criteria or how to join
Limited details on branch count, ATM network, or service area coverage
best_for: Greensboro, NC area residents seeking personalized credit union banking services
Individuals eligible for membership seeking competitive auto loan rates
Members planning for retirement through tax-advantaged IRA/KEOGH accounts
Borrowers with fair to good credit interested in building credit through credit cards
diagnosis: Internal Revenue Employees Federal Credit Union is best for Greensboro-area residents and eligible members seeking competitive lending rates and personalized credit union banking. Primary caveat: membership eligibility appears restricted, service area is geographically limited, and the institution is significantly smaller than national banks, which may limit convenience and product availability.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
